title: What To Do About Bad Practitioners
filename: What To Do About Bad Practitioners.en.vtt
excerpt: Hello everyone I don't know if you saw the post that I did on the weekend a lot of people shared it about this crazy case where someone had a lip treatment at a clinic and then they'd had a problem a few months later six months later and they caught a got in contact with that cli…

segments.text: hello everyone I don't know if you saw the post that I did on the weekend a lot of people shared it about this crazy case where someone had a lip treatment at a clinic and then they'd had a problem a few months later six months later and they caught a got in contact with that clinic to try and get their lip sorted out which I think reading between the lines have become hard and lumpy and the clinic had basically been incredibly rude to them the person who was on this message had basically accused the person with a lip problem of it being a sexually transmitted disease which is total nonsense and basically just trying to insult her in the process of refusing to help her so I mean this kind of thing gets good practitioners rightfully really angry because we all know how we respond in those situations which is you tend we all most medical professionals will go overboard to make sure that person's helped and in fact we also have the we have a tendency to blame

ourselves long before we start to blame the patient so we have this there's a sense of like what if it's me what if I miss something I must make sure that I sort this patient out and the last thing you allowed to come into your head normally is us as the patient being weird or you know they're tricky people you we tend to take all the responsibility which is acting exactly what a health care professional should do but in this particular case a lot of people a lot of us often feel well what can I do to stop this because it's so unfair that these bad practitioners are going around harming people and getting away with it and they obviously have thriving businesses but they're not looking after people what can I do so I thought I'd give it a little bit of thought about what the sort of things you can actually do to contribute towards improving the system so they're the way I've broken it down they're essentially only three things there are kind of three ways that you could tackle this and the first is kind of a long-term thing which I think we should all be doing which is it's looking at the forces that might regulate our industry so there are lots

of sort of people who want to try to improve things and make it safer and do something to try and make that a more solid offering because at the moment things like the JCC P it's a voluntary organize a it's very early days whether it's going to work on art is unknown but maybe getting involved with something like that 5 you know 7 years time you might actually have been part of something that actually does make the industry safer for those people are uninformed so that's one particular option it's al it's a long-term game nothing's going to change very quickly but that's one option and the next thing which is quite a common one is you could go out and troll the people who have bad practitioners and you can leave comments on their Instagram and you can kind of tell them that they're rubbish and that they're not safe and they're not injecting in the right place and they're not aspirating and they're not wearing gloves and they're not you know you shouldn't be treating drunk people all of those things are real things that I've seen happen which so one of your options is to go and do that I think it might make you feel better in the short term and weirdly it's really

it's actually kind of helps them in the short term as well because engagement on these posts drives organic reach so the more comments there are on practitioners who you disagree with the better it is for them and they actually more people get to see them and they become famous and as you know you can all you all know of non-medical jecht is who are actually quite famous and often are very bad as well and but they're famous it doesn't matter there are enough people who know about them that they fill their clinics with people who want to be part of the fame so we need to think about whether it's good thing to report to troll people on their Facebook groups on their Facebook pages and their Instagram pages but that is an option and some people you get a quick release of feeling like at least you've said something and but I don't think it'll do much and the next thing is you can report when you're involved with the case and there's a complication I do actually would encourage this because there's a big movement to collect data on exactly what the risks are in our industry to try and make it better and so the Jai CCP in particular are trying to pull data from the mrj and from other bodies healthier and you know damaged

NHS England and all sorts of bodies are trying to get this data I think save face by the way discovered also collecting data now hopefully this data all going into one place will become a force for regulation and also it's long term so you're not going to get you're not going to report someone who's caused a complication and immediately have their clinic closed down if it's the kind of thing that could happen in a in a well-run clinic to like there's an element of bad luck and it might be different if they're actually and I've breaking really severe rules in terms of health public health and but those are probably a bit rarer so you can report people and the final one is actually the one i'm i actually think we've got them the most control over and that's always a good thing when you have control over something you'll feel a lot better and that is to try and put stuff out there to try and explain to your audience what they really should expect from a good treatment and i love this one because it's gonna force everyone to get better if you really start to think about what patients need to be safe and you start

to realize that the way for you to win and build your practice is to create a safer environment and a better offering for patients and then you communicate that to everyone you're going to do better your business will be better and for this to work you've got to believe in the best of people and what i mean by that is given the truth about the risk between summit between a you know a well-trained health care professional and a badly trained of non health care professional in terms of attitudes like were given the truth between the spectrum of people who might be doing the procedures that we do and people are tending are going to tend to make a good decision for themselves if they have all the facts at their disposal now one of the problems which i realized recently is if you're good at marketing on instagram and you have no roof no ability to review someone you're basically pumping out a propaganda feed which you can edit so anyone who comes on and says you really messed up my lips you can delete that comment you can ban them from your page and you get on with your propaganda stream and actually that there's no way for someone who's

marketing on instagram to be held to account you can build a thriving business and have a really good looking kind of looks like a well-established great first-rate clinic on the instagram feed and then the reality is something totally different so that's a big problem for us but what you can do is you can make your choice a better choice so in your own marketing put out stuff other than just before and afters we all know before and afters work but actually put out something about your ethos what you promise to do for people when they're in a bad such if anything happens and how can you help them what do you do to reduce risk and what do you do when things go do go wrong what kind of things can go wrong and what are you doing every day to reduce the risk of it now I know this is sometimes scary because sometimes we feel if we're completely transparent that people will think this is happening all the time you'll put it you're put people off having treatment but I don't think that's the case I think people are clever enough to understand that if you do a it that if there are risks that some practitioners are focused on that

they want to reduce that risk and they want to be prepared to solve the risk and the more you talk about that the more it's going to become one of the factors that people choose in terms of which practitioner they see so the thing I'm calling for apart from all the other things you can do is to really get stuck in with selling the real benefits of being a health care practitioner and what I love about this is it is gonna make you have to think hard about what that is that's gonna make you a better practitioner yourself you're gonna communicate it more often you're gonna feel safer in your practice you're actually going to get safer and the choice will become obvious now I don't know we don't have power over what legislation is gonna happen and how quickly we'll have but you do have power and to change the mindset of the people who are looking for treatment really quickly by just pumping out really good-quality guidance on how to get a treatment that'll make you happy and that will also keep you safe and the practitioner and a whole package that comes with the offering that you have rather than just focusing on the before and after which as you know there's

some selective you know we can all take before and after and believe that is that it's a bit two-dimensional we all know there's a before there's a during there's an after in terms of the person themselves the risks what can happen and so I think we need to as a collective really start to communicate much more about the benefits because I know practitioners you know you see these unsafe practitioners injecting and they're casual and that you know the rest of us who worry think I can't believe that they're doing that when I inject I'm really worrying I'm aspirating I know I my blood runs cold all the time because I'm concentrating so hard not to make a mistake and then you see people doing the opposite I also know that so many of you will be up at 11 o'clock at night responding to messages often you'll go out and see patients late at night I've heard of emergency reversals going on it midnight because you're putting any extra effort to keep people safe and actually I don't think many people know that I just don't think that people are fully aware of how much effort

and time will go into making sure that your patients are safe and I think we should all tell people more you should all tell people more about what you're prepared to do to keep them safe your commitment to safety and the way that having a governing body actually makes you have to do that and how many times a day do we all think about your registration I've got to do this because I don't I you know my registration is important to me I know that's a massive factor for so many health care professionals and it just isn't if you're not on a registered body so we talk about that more often and actually drive people to make a rational choice so at the bottom of this request is believe that people are rational and if they know the truth they will make a good decision and I believe that of most people some people you can't argue with they'll be but they're a minority most people want to keep themselves safe they just don't know the choice that they're making so and that's kind of my thought for the day given that little mini viral thing that's gone round with the lady who was it was treated awfully by that practice but and let me know we think if you

think that's a good idea let me know and tag anyone who might be interested who might agree with this and share this post if you think more people should see it and once again keep the questions coming in I have got a few questions to get through over the last few days I have got a little bit behind I've been really busy but that's no excuse I'll be back I'll do another Facebook live tomorrow I hope that's interesting and thanks everyone for watching
